entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: Country in Africa where the study on bio-reclamation of degraded lands was conducted

entities.name: MARADI
entities.description: Region in Niger where data was collected from women farmers

entities.name: ZINDER
entities.description: Region in Niger where data was collected from women farmers

entities.name: DEGRADED LATERITIC SOILS
entities.description: Type of soil composing about 50% of the land surface in Niger

entities.name: BIO-RECLAMATION OF DEGRADED LAND
entities.description: Technology used to restore degraded lands and improve livelihoods for rural women

entities.name: LAND-ENHANCING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Broad category of technologies, including BDL, designed to improve land quality and productivity

entities.name: HOUSEHOLD INCOME
entities.description: Economic measure used to evaluate the impact of BDL technology on women farmers

entities.name: ENDOGENOUS SWITCHING REGRESSION
entities.description: Econometric model used to estimate the impact of BDL technology on household income while accounting for selection bias

entities.name: PROBIT MODEL
entities.description: Statistical model used to analyze the determinants of participation in the BDL program

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: Policy goal aimed at ensuring access to sufficient and nutritious food in rural dryland areas

entities.name: POVERTY ALLEVIATION
entities.description: Policy goal aimed at reducing poverty in rural dryland areas

entities.name: PURCHASING POWER
entities.description: The financial capacity of women farmers to acquire goods and services
